items.title: Fuel Prices Show Inflation Is Still a Risk for Markets
items.description: While oil prices have fallen, the cost of refined products like gasoline and diesel are proving to be much stickier.

items.title: Rain-Soaked China Braces For 'Gargantuan' Typhoon
items.description: China is facing more torrential rain and flooding this weekend from a massive typhoon tracking toward the country’s east coast, following recent severe storms that led to deaths and crop damage. (Sour

items.title: India’s Wartime Buying Spree Leaves Behind a Cooking Gas Surplus
items.description: India’s frenzied efforts to counter disruptions in cooking gas supplies over the past months, while cargoes remained trapped in the Persian Gulf, has left state-run fuel retailers with the opposite pr

items.title: Nigerian Stocks Vault Past South Korea’s Kospi to Claim World’s Top Returns
items.description: Nigerian equities have overtaken South Korea’s to hand investors the highest dollar-based returns this year, as souring sentiment on artificial-intelligence stocks pushes the Asian nation’s world-beat

items.title: Serbia Holds Rates as Election Welfare Plans Signal Price Risks
items.description: Serbia kept borrowing costs unchanged for a record 22nd month as a government plan for cash handouts before this year’s general election may add to inflation pressures.

items.title: Erste Shares to Rally 30% on Poland Growth, Goldman Sachs Says
items.description: Erste Group Bank AG shares are set to rally 30%, driven by growth from the lender’s expansion in Poland, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc.

items.title: Barclays Readies $875 Million Debt Sale for Buyout of Senior Plc
items.description: Barclays Plc is preparing to sell about $875 million of debt financing used to fund a takeover of UK aerospace supplier Senior Plc.

items.title: Defense Valuations Yet to Peak as Orders Surge, BNP Banker Says
items.description: European defense sector valuations still have plenty of upside, with government pledges to boost military spending now translating into orders, according to a top banker at BNP Paribas SA.

items.title: Turkey’s Vestel Hires Advisers for Dollar Bond Restructuring
items.description: Turkey’s Vestel Elektronik has hired advisers Houlihan Lokey for restructuring talks with bondholders, at the same time as the struggling electronics manufacturer renegotiates loans with local banks.

items.title: US Strikes Iran A Second Day; AI Rotation Trade on Watch | The Asia Trade 7/9/2026

items.description: In this episode of The Deal, Hilary details for Jason and Alex the process of starting a league where players were partners from the jump and why Mark Walter and Billie Jean King were the right people

items.title: BlackRock’s Jewell Sees AI Capex Stable for Two to Three Years
items.description: Commitments for AI spending should sustain the investment theme around the technology for two to three years, even as tech giants gradually turn cash-flow negative and start raising debt to fund their

items.title: Hugo Boss Board Rejects Frasers Bid as Not Reflecting Potential
items.description: Hugo Boss AG recommended shareholders reject Frasers Group Plc’s takeover offer, saying the bid fails to capture the German fashion company’s long-term value.

items.title: UK’s Capita Sinks on Profit Hit From Pension Contract Failures
items.description: Capita Plc tumbled after it said profit and cash flow will be hit by its failure to deliver on a contract to manage Britain’s civil service pension plan.

items.title: Oil Fluctuates After Second Day of US Strikes on Iran
items.description: Oil swung as traders assessed the outlook for Middle Eastern crude supplies after US forces conducted a second day of strikes against Iran. Vessel-tracking data on Thursday showed a drop-off in transi

items.title: China's Tech Stack Outshines its Economy: 3-Minutes MLIV
items.description: Anna Edwards, Guy Johnson and Paul Dobson break down today's key themes for analysts and investors on "Bloomberg: The Opening Trade." (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Thailand and Malaysia Reach Deal to End Seafood Trade Spat
items.description: Thailand and Malaysia have resolved a seafood trade dispute, paving the way for fishery trade between the two countries to return to normal soon, their leaders said.

items.title: How South Korea's SK Hynix Became Central to the AI Boom
items.description:
SK Hynix may not be a household name, but the South Korean memory chipmaker is one of the top names in the hottest corner of the stock market.

Ahead of its US listing, Winnie Hsu looks at how the onc

items.title: France Plans €2 Billion to Cut Fertilizer Imports Dependence
items.description: France will invest €2 billion ($2.3 billion) to cut dependence on fertilizer imports in an effort to shield farmers from future supply disruptions.

items.title: SK Hynix’s Stock Swings Challenge Arbitrage Bets on New ADRs
items.description: Arbitrage desks looking to trade SK Hynix Inc.’s new American depository receipts are dusting off playbooks from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. But many say the comparison only goes so far.
